reviews.review_description: This is a good game that could be a great game. The basic mechanics of it works really well, and it's fun to play. The problem is too little player control. And by control, I mean, control over one's own life. It's okay to have missions if someone wants to take a mission or they're not sure what they can do or should do. Ultimately, the player should be pretty much autonomous. The goal can still be to upgrade your ship to perform better as well as make enough money to pay back your debt. But other than that, let the player go where they want, salvage what they want. Like, initially, due to storage and fuel, they operate locally picking up low level salvage, but as they upgrade their ship, they're able to go further and further out, getting the more valuable salvage (also, if the player runs out of fuel, emergency services should cut into their profits). And as well allow the player to actually pay back their debt. So they can spend their money to upgrade the equipment or they can spend their money to cut down on what they owe. You can even throw in an interest charge to motivate them to want to pay some of the debt in order to prevent the amount due to rise. But the bottom line is just doing one mission after another is boring and monotonous... Like you didn't really accomplish anything.

reviews.review_title: Game is now UNPLAYAVlBLE- Game not work
reviews.review_description: I used to love this game. Reinstalled it to play some more and it loads the cut scenes and such but any level and I mean any mission Doesn't load it is like a black screen, I have tried everything out of my 60+ games this is the only one giving me any issues. I have reinstalled and cleared data and as we no there is no cloud backup so as far as I am concerned this game is unplayable. Sad too, that mean we get our money back? Was this abandoned.

developer_response.body: Hi NoodlesInVR - sorry to hear you've  been having troubles with Space Salvage on your headset.  Please could you email support@fruitysystems.com and we will try to help fix the issue.

reviews.review_description:
The game has great atmosphere and I like that I can play from a seated position.  It seems like upgrades are locked behind a license / progression limit, which is fine to an extent but if I want to grind lower level missions to out-level main content I think that should be an option.
The primary issue I have is that the controls are all or nothing between thumb-sticks and grip for throttle and yoke.  Throttle locks at the last position when you let go in grip mode, which I usually don't want.  Hitting a button before release to lock would be nice, or even allowing grip yoke and stick throttle would be nice.  As it is, if you enable thumb-sticks you can no longer grasp the yoke or the throttle.  It's a lot of preferences to set up, but allowing players to customize the controls more precisely would go a long way toward eliminating the frustrating aspects of an otherwise enjoyable game.
Nice but way too much expensive for so tiny contents. Pitty...

If you’re expecting Hardspace Shipbreaker in VR you’re going to be disappointed. If you’re expecting an arcade style space job simulator with basic flight mechanics, basic combat, and a tongue in cheek story, you’ll have a good time.

developer_response.body: Glad you had a good time :)

reviews.review_description: I like most of the controls even though it can be a bit clunky, but the combat doesn't feel good with the limited rotation of the main laser and the annoying to use railgun. I feel like sometimes the game fails to tell you important information (such as the threat of rockets in the tower construction level), and that the mission where you need to defend a cargo ship is an unwarranted and unfair difficulty spike. I really don't want to have to grind out attempts of that mission but any further progress is locked behind it and I'm starting to wish I could turn off the ship voice.

developer_response.body: Hi FamilyConrad - thanks for your feedback.  We agree that the cargo escort level, Mimosa, is pretty tough.  We'll have a look at easing the difficulty off a bit for it.

reviews.review_description: The update to Quest 3 is a true success! The graphics are of exceptional quality, incredibly sharp, and the gameplay is simply perfect. One truly feels immersed in our little spacecraft. Unlike many space games, there is no risk of motion sickness. However, the only small remark I could make is that we would like to see more varied missions to avoid the feeling of repetition. I am convinced that this game will achieve great success in the months to come. I sincerely hope that the developers will continue to work on this gem to make it even more exceptional. I don't know if you've added it to the game, but I want to buy a fire extinguisher to put out the fire in my cabin, also some keys to make a few repairs lol😂 and why not integrate a radio station to listen to music in space?

Super chill cockpit tasking game! Really feels like you are on the job out there. Game allows you to work at your own pace and snoop around the environments for extra loot. RPG elements of upgrading your ship's tech, aesthetics and getting promoted feels solid and there seems to be an enormous amount of content here for the price. graphics are cartoony, but the world is fun and immersive. There are no glitches so far and the frame rate is smooth. So thank you devs for taking your time cooking this one before release! The piloting is easy to grasp and having the flight stick on the left only took a few minutes to get used to! As soon as you get your basic laser, you can see why they did it that way. I love my new dayjob, scouring space for valuable scrap and fighting off occasional pesky enemies. Could easily sink many hours into this cathartic game loop. No rushing you through the levels, you are allowed and encouraged to explore at your own pace! Only two things would make this game really shine IMO: 1) I need some kind of thruster sound fx. Movement ids too quiet for me.  2) It kind of takes away from the "laboring" aspect of the game when the salvage is just automatically sucked into the ship when you are close enough to it. We need an option to lock on to it manually and then also another action to trigger the tractor beam. the it would really feel like we are out here working! Maybe even having to cut some pieces with the laser before salvaging them! But as is, this is money VERY well spent and a super chill, meaty, immersive sci fi/cockpit game. Good job devs! 
UPDATE: thanks devs for responding! I have tried the game with headphones and the engine sounds great with them on. Also I am a few hrs in on the game now and I see even more cockpit interactivity coming into play! Wish more youtubers were talking about this title, it deserves more attention!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback Meatballmachine - very glad you are enjoying the game.

I've added your two suggestions as feature requests for a future update.

reviews.review_description:
If you are looking for a super chill seated vr game that gets you right into the action, then I recommend this game. 

The game is mission structured with very short load times (even on quest 2) and very easy to learn and pick up. 

The controls were surprisingly intuitive and easy to learn, after a couple short tutorials I was already making quick turns and and getting accurate shots with the ships weapon. 

This is a very personal preference but I do wish that there was stronger haptics/vibration when gripping the ships control sticks, for me that always adds a level of immersion and make its feel like my hands are actually interacting with objects. 

I highly recommend that if you play this game to use a chair with solid or decent sized arm rests at a good height, as it makes it much more comftable to control your ship by sliding your arms back and forth. I have one of them racing gaming desk chairs and it's perfect for this kind of experience.

developer_response.body:
Hi slide - glad you liked the game.  

Thanks for the detailed feedback.  I'm happy that you found the controls intuitive - we spent a lot of time working on them!

Thanks for the suggestion about increasing the haptic feedback... I've added a feature request to include an option to increase the haptic feedback in a future update.

reviews.review_title: What a cute fun space game
reviews.review_description: Love this one!!

developer_response.body: Very glad you found it fun TheRedKingQC ... that was our goal ;)

reviews.review_description:
This is a game with a unique style and interesting characters! 

They have a good variety of gameplay modes across levels, such as; collecting required items, combat, careful navigation with checkpoints, challenges for players who want to compare their skills to other players, and more! I've played all 3 storylines and I had a blast!

They also have a comfort mode that helps a lot with motion sickness too, a much appreciated option to have!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot - glad you're enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description:
This game is really fun and perfect for seated play.

TWENTY FIVE PERCENT OFF:

https://alex82•bio•link

I was looking for a different game, not focused on shooting, and here it is! Very nice graphics, physics and lots of fun! I highly recommend this one. Please add more environments and planets to visit.  Thanks!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! We're really glad you enjoyed Space Salvage. We will work on some updates with more parts of the galaxy to explore, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: Space Salvage is a great VR game
reviews.review_description:
Piloting the spacecraft feels very satisfying, the controls are responsive and the flying is very smooth- the more you upgrade the better the flying experience gets. There is a good variety in objectives and places you visit; from trespassing corporate crush sites, dodging your way through a blazing meteorite shower, breaking into a high security corporate warehouse to even  blowing up a star - just to name a few scenarios - and all that in the name of glorious space bucks!

I had a great time broadcasting the game on a TV and passing the headset around with my mates, it was very fun. Apart from the structured missions there are also races and combat challenges where you can compete for the highest place on the leaderboard, make sure to upgrade your spacecraft to reach its full potential

developer_response.body: Great! - Very glad you're enjoying it.

reviews.review_description: A wonderful game that I have been excited to play since first playing the demo. It had a really fun premise and a fun sense of humour. The art style is a fun cartoon shader style, the music is immersive at quite relaxing at many points and he game play is fun and changes between levels. This maybe for slightly.more advanced VR users as there is a lot of movement in this but you get used to it. Overall great game from a great team.

developer_response.body: Thanks.  Really glad you're enjoying the humour and the varied gameplay.

reviews.review_description: Not by a long shot. You cant even configure the controls to make it smooth.

developer_response.body:
Hi - sorry if you thought this was going to be Elite Dangerous.... we love that game, but it just couldn't work on a Quest 2 or 3 running standalone.  We've gone for a more focussed, narrative driven game instead of an open and-box universe game.

On the controls we are working on an update which will offer different options for reconfiguring your movement controls which will hopefully help you.

reviews.review_title: Even just the demo tells me it's great
reviews.review_description: I played the demo like 5 times, I know that the game is great. I will improve this review with more details, although I'm sure the stars will stay at 5, at least from me.

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed the demo omayops - I'm sure you'll enjoy the full game too!

reviews.review_description: Great style, great story, love the characters a lot. This is not a space sim, it's a lot more cartoony and story oriented. One thing I really like is the size of the maps and skyboxes. Highly recommended.

The idea of space salvage could be an interesting setting. The flight- and control-concept is far away from realistic. It is more or less comic style and becomes boring and repetitive after an hour. 

Like many VR content on quest it is a bit the wow effect for VR newbies and after all no quality, no atmosphere and not something you want to return to. 

If you look for a „game“ to showcase VR to new people and family friendly- go ahead.

If you look for for a space-sim with story, atmosphere and excitement based on content and missions then spare this one.

developer_response.body: Hi nemezir.2024 - sorry that you didn't find the game as engaging as we would have hoped.  How far into the game did you get?  There is more variety in the mission objectives as you get further in and purchase more upgrades.

reviews.review_description:
So far Iv found it easy to get into and for me as an older gamer it fits perfectly into the niche of VR highly recommend it, cheers and have fun.

🤩 b i o .  L I n k  t r a m p s t y l e. 🌰

developer_response.body: Thanks Trampstyle - glad you like it!
